title: Employee Data Handling Guidelines – Q1 2026
body:
EMPLOYEE DATA HANDLING GUIDELINES – Q1 2026

PURPOSE
This compliance note summarizes essential employee data handling requirements under GDPR and SOC2 Trust Services Criteria. All engineers, product teams, and infrastructure staff must understand and apply these guidelines when processing, storing, or transmitting employee data in any system or workflow.

GDPR REQUIREMENTS FOR EMPLOYEE DATA

Scope of Application: <cite index="1-11">GDPR applies to US or multinational companies that have any employees in the EU</cite>. <cite index="9-5">Personal data is defined broadly as any information related to an identified or identifiable person; this can include names, emails, phone numbers, IDs, or even indirect identifiers like online user IDs</cite>.

Key Handling Obligations:
- <cite index="4-1">Implement the principles of data protection "by design and default" in all data processing activities and projects</cite>. This means privacy considerations must be embedded in system design from the start, not added later.
- <cite index="6-10">Limit access to employees' personal data and keep it secure</cite>. Use role-based access controls; engineers should only access employee data required for their specific function.
- <cite index="9-17,9-18">Organizations must use "state of the art" security measures like encryption for personal data wherever possible. If your role involves handling personal data files, you should ensure you're using approved, secure tools (for example, an enterprise cloud storage instead of a personal USB)</cite>.
- <cite index="9-3">Data breaches must be reported to authorities within 72 hours in many cases</cite>. If you discover a breach or unauthorized access, report it immediately to security@company and your manager.

Data Subject Rights: <cite index="7-2">Employees have the right to access, rectify, or erase their personal data held by employers</cite>. When you receive a data subject access request internally, escalate to Legal & Compliance immediate
